display.purpose: Major page titles and brand-facing demo surfaces.

headline.purpose: Section headers and prominent panel titles.

title.purpose: Card, task, and review section headings.

label.purpose: Badges, form labels, navigation labels, and compact status text.

ambient-card.purpose: Major cards and feature panels only.

flat-panel.purpose: Nested surfaces, task cards, badges, inputs, and side panels.

motion.reducedMotion: Any future motion must provide a prefers-reduced-motion alternative.

badge.character: Pill labels that name state explicitly.

card.character: Border-first product surfaces with soft tonal layering.

task-board.character: Four-column progress board with labeled status columns and proof-oriented task cards.

dos: Keep workflows clear, role-aware, and task-first.
Use System Blue for action, focus, navigation, links, selected items, and progress.
Use the blueprint as the global web background, with clean white cards and forms above it.
Pair every status color with text, count, shape, or placement.
Use border and tonal layer before shadow.
donts: Do not make Kickstart feel like generic SaaS, a childish app, swipe-app energy, a cluttered dashboard, or cold corporate software.
Do not use red as the general brand accent; reserve it for reject, delete, error, danger, and critical warnings.
Do not put blueprint texture inside every application card or form.
Do not rely only on color for application, task, review, or progress states.
Do not use decorative gradients, glassmorphism, marketing metric blocks, repeated uppercase eyebrows, or side-stripe callouts as default patterns.
